# Changelog — 2026-04-13
*Generated by Agent 14a — Assumption Extractor*

---

## Daily Narrative

April 13 was primarily an operational day for C2A2 with critical literature search results returned. Four specialist agent sessions ran — some on schedule, some catching up from missed weekend runs — producing 14 new proposals across 8 thinkers (Levin, Friston, Stump, Fredrickson, Carroll, Arkani-Hamed, Wolfram). The wiki daily run failed with a 401 authentication error for the fourth consecutive day, freezing PRS counts at 76 and creating a growing backlog. The morning briefing system correctly identified the 23+ proposal review backlog as the system's biggest bottleneck, but no alerting mechanism caught the multi-day wiki pipeline failure. The self-awareness pipeline (14a/14b) completed its second cycle, surfacing 3 new assumptions and 4 new presumptions from the day's operational patterns. More significantly, Agents 15a and 15b completed independent literature searches on all 25 queued items (11 assumptions + 14 presumptions), and Agent 14a has now reconciled the results, discovering significant challenges to core architectural assumptions — particularly concerning role-assignment bias, multi-agent coordination overhead, and the validity of cross-tradition signals.

---

## Changes Detected

CHANGE-2026-04-13-001:
  Type: Operational — Specialist agent output
  Description: Four specialist agent sessions produced 14 proposals total:
    - Levin/Friston (Monday agents): 6 proposals (PROP-001 through PROP-006) — neurobots, CHARLES-D, top-down membrane potential, active inference reasoning, predictive coding survey, differential flatness
    - Stump/Fredrickson (catch-up from Thursday): 3 proposals (PROP-007 through PROP-009) — Thomistic virtues, trust and stranger connection, inflammation and positivity resonance
    - Carroll/Arkani-Hamed (catch-up from Friday): 3 proposals (PROP-010 through PROP-012) — emergence taxonomy, kinematic flow, hidden zeros
    - Wolfram (catch-up from Saturday): 2 proposals (PROP-013 through PROP-014) — foundation tool/CAG, consciousness/AI Q&A
  Impact: 14 proposals added to pending/. Review backlog now estimated at 37+ proposals (23 prior + 14 new).
  Related decisions: DECISION-003 (operational execution)
  Notes: Catch-up pattern (3 of 4 sessions were backfill from missed days) suggests the specialist agent schedule was interrupted over the weekend.

CHANGE-2026-04-13-002:
  Type: Infrastructure failure — Wiki daily run
  Description: The C282 wiki daily run failed completely with a 401 authentication error ("Invalid authentication credentials"). The morning briefing confirmed no successful wiki daily runs since April 9 — a 4-day processing gap.
  Impact: PRS counts frozen at 76. No proposals processed from pending/ to wiki. No pattern detection runs on new material. Cumulative drift growing.
  Related decisions: None (no decision made to address this)
  Notes: This is the operational manifestation of PRESUMPTION-013 (infrastructure resilience). The system lacks alerting for multi-day pipeline failures.

CHANGE-2026-04-13-003:
  Type: Self-awareness pipeline — 14a/14b second cycle
  Description: Agents 14a and 14b completed their second run (first was 2026-04-10). Extracted 3 new assumptions (ASSUMPTION-011 through 013) and surfaced 4 new presumptions (PRESUMPTION-011 through 014) from today's operational activity. All 7 new items queued for literature search.
  Impact: Total assumptions: 13 (was 10). Total presumptions: 14 (was 10). Literature search queue: 25 items (was 18).
  Related decisions: DECISION-005 (14a/14b pipeline), DECISION-006 (15a/15b queue)
  Notes: The most significant presumption surfaced today is PRESUMPTION-013 (infrastructure resilience), which is already being violated by the 4-day wiki pipeline failure. PRESUMPTION-014 (cross-tradition signal validity) is the deepest epistemic challenge — questioning whether C2A2's core output is structurally meaningful.

CHANGE-2026-04-13-004:
  Type: Self-awareness pipeline — 15a/15b literature searches and 14a reconciliation
  Description: Agents 15a (supportive search) and 15b (adversarial search) completed independent literature searches on all 25 queued items (11 assumptions + 14 presumptions) on 2026-04-13. Agent 14a has now reconciled both search results per the provenance protocol, determining final epistemic status for each item.
  Impact: 
    - 2 assumptions now SUPPORTED (ASSUMPTION-005)
    - 2 assumptions PARTIALLY-SUPPORTED (ASSUMPTION-006, ASSUMPTION-010)
    - 7 assumptions PARTIALLY-CHALLENGED (ASSUMPTION-003, 004, 007, 008, 011, 013; mix of support and challenge)
    - 2 assumptions CONTESTED (ASSUMPTION-009, ASSUMPTION-012; strong evidence both for and against)
    - 12 presumptions PARTIALLY-CHALLENGED
    - 2 presumptions CHALLENGED (PRESUMPTION-006, PRESUMPTION-007, PRESUMPTION-011)
  Risk elevation: Two items elevated to CRITICAL/HIGH risk:
    - PRESUMPTION-002 (Thousand Brains transfer): CRITICAL — entire C2A2 redesign depends on this transfer validity; literature search found only moderate evidence of transfer success
    - PRESUMPTION-014 (cross-tradition signal validity): HIGH — core output quality depends on agents' ability to distinguish structural from surface connections; literature search found this distinction is empirically hard to validate
    - PRESUMPTION-013 (infrastructure resilience): MEDIUM-HIGH — already being actively violated (4-day wiki failure with no alerting)
  Related decisions: DECISION-005, DECISION-006, DECISION-007 (provenance protocol)
  Notes: Literature search revealed pervasive challenges to architectural assumptions. Role assignment (FOR/AGAINST agents) may amplify bias rather than prevent it (ASSUMPTION-003, PRESUMPTION-005). Multi-agent coordination overhead likely exceeds scaling assumptions (ASSUMPTION-004). Philosophical arguments suggest AI agents cannot truly "instantiate" traditions (ASSUMPTION-007). Most troubling: core value proposition (cross-tradition signals) rests on unvalidated assumption that agents can reliably distinguish structural from superficial connections (PRESUMPTION-014, ASSUMPTION-013). Recommend urgent risk mitigation planning.

## Evening Addendum (14a/14b third cycle — post-pipeline)
*Generated by Agent 14a/14b — evening run, after lit search pipeline completion*

### Narrative

The most architecturally significant event of April 13 occurred in the afternoon: **the self-awareness pipeline completed its first full cycle.** All 25 queued items passed through 15a → 15b → 15c, producing 3 INCORPORATE, 15 MONITOR, and 7 REVISE dispositions. This is the completion of Stage 1 in the developmental maturity model. The evening 14a/14b run then analyzed the pipeline's own operation and surfaced 3 new assumptions (ASSUMPTION-014 through 016) and 4 new presumptions (PRESUMPTION-015 through 018) about the pipeline itself — making this the first instance of C2A2's self-awareness system becoming self-aware of its own self-awareness process.

CHANGE-2026-04-13-005:
  Type: Self-awareness pipeline — First full cycle completion
  Description: Agents 15a and 15b completed independent literature searches on all 25 queued items. Agent 15c dispositioned all 25 items. Results: 3 INCORPORATE (ASSUMPTION-005, 009, 012), 15 MONITOR, 7 REVISE. This completes the Stage 1 benchmark in the developmental maturity model.
  Impact: C2A2 now has a validated premises register (3 items), a monitor queue (15 items for Agent 15d), and a revision flags list (7 items for Tom's review). The self-awareness pipeline is operational end-to-end.
  Related decisions: DECISION-006 (15a/15b), DECISION-012 (15c), DECISION-009 (Stage 1 benchmark)
  Notes: Pipeline identified significant systemic risks — strongest clustering around role-based bias amplification, AI agency assumptions, and infrastructure failure. Two data consistency discrepancies noted (monitor_queue.md has 2 fewer entries than expected).

CHANGE-2026-04-13-006:
  Type: Self-awareness pipeline — 14a/14b third cycle (meta-level)
  Description: Agents 14a and 14b completed their third run, this time analyzing the lit search pipeline's own operation. Extracted 3 new assumptions (ASSUMPTION-014 through 016) and surfaced 4 new presumptions (PRESUMPTION-015 through 018). All 7 items queued for 15a/15b.
  Impact: Total assumptions: 16 (was 13). Total presumptions: 18 (was 14). Lit search queue: 32 items total (25 complete + 7 new QUEUED).
  Related decisions: DECISION-005 (14a/14b pipeline)
  Notes: Most significant finding is PRESUMPTION-015 (self-referential circularity) — the pipeline evaluated claims about its own structure using its own structure. Also notable: ASSUMPTION-015 (pipeline acknowledged its own bias risk but operated anyway, implicitly assuming biased operation > no operation).

CHANGE-2026-04-13-007:
  Type: Operational — Evening sync to Chat
  Description: The evening cowork-to-chat sync delivered a detailed summary to the "Morning walk check-in" conversation on claude.ai, priming 4 discussion topics: wiki auth fix, Phase 2a pause, PRESUMPTION-002 de-risking, and PRESUMPTION-007 operationalization.
  Impact: Chat now has context for morning walk discussion. Assumes Chat retains context reliably across sessions (see PRESUMPTION-018).
  Related decisions: None (operational practice)

## Open Questions — Evening Updates

OPEN-013:
  Date raised: 2026-04-13
  Question: How should the self-awareness pipeline handle self-referential evaluation? When 15a/15b evaluate claims about 15a/15b's own design (ASSUMPTION-003, PRESUMPTION-005), the results may be circular. Should these items be evaluated by an independent mechanism?
  Context: PRESUMPTION-015. The pipeline evaluated ASSUMPTION-003 and PRESUMPTION-005 — both about the FOR/AGAINST structure — using the FOR/AGAINST structure itself.
  Related decisions: DECISION-006
  Status: Open — epistemically significant

OPEN-014:
  Date raised: 2026-04-13
  Question: Should 15c's disposition heuristics be calibrated after the first full cycle? The current run produced 7 REVISE items. Tom should review whether the heuristics are appropriately calibrated or whether the threshold for REVISE is too strict or lenient.
  Context: OPEN-008 (calibration question). The first full cycle provides the data needed to answer this question. ASSUMPTION-014 questions whether the framework itself is right.
  Related decisions: DECISION-012
  Status: Open — answerable now (first cycle data available)

## Decision Index — No New Decisions

No new architectural decisions were made on 2026-04-13. The day was operational (proposal generation, lit search pipeline completion) with no design discussions observed in session transcripts. However, the implicit decision to pause Phase 2a (ASSUMPTION-016) may become a formal decision after the morning walk discussion.
